sections.paragraph:
μετακόσμιος, ον (κόσμος; Diog. L. 10, 89; Plut.; Philo, Somn. 1, 184, Conf. Lingu. 134) between/after the world(s); elsewh. an Epicurean expr., denoting the space betw. heaven and earth (intermundia: Cicero, Nat. Deor. 1, 8, 18); here prob. what is to come after this world (in time) Dg 12:9 (text uncertain; s. ἁρμόζω 2).—DELG s.v. κόσμος.

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

expr. expr. = expression

betw. betw. = between

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 639
